title: Epoch feeds are not viable on bee-js 11 — SDK support is vestigial
content:
## Epoch feeds: SDK gap blocks adoption

Swarm epoch feeds (paper §4.3.4) index SOC feeds by `(time, level)` instead of sequence number, giving a cold reader O(log age) lookup of the latest-as-of-time update — directly useful for the cross-Bee `publishPublicProfile` staleness we see on 2.7.x (receivers serve pre-update versions for minutes).

But they are not implementable today:

- **bee-js 11.1.1**: the `Epoch { time, level }` type exists, but `findNextIndex`, `updateFeedWithReference`, and `downloadFeedUpdate` all take a sequence-`FeedIndex`. Epoch support is vestigial.
- **Bee 2.7.x HTTP**: `/feeds/{owner}/{topic}` is sequence-only.
- **Custom path**: would require bypassing feed helpers and manipulating SOCs directly — epoch lookup algorithm, custom chunk address derivation per level, grid traversal reader. Feasible but significant scope.

**Decision:** DEFER. Reconsider when bee-js or Bee exposes epoch lookup natively. Not worth custom chunk-layer work right now.
description: bee-js 11.1.1 defines the Epoch type but every feed function takes a sequence-FeedIndex, and Bee 2.7.x /feeds HTTP is sequence-only. Defer until native.
